does: This paper introduces VideoScore2, a multi-dimensional video evaluation framework that assesses generated videos across three explicit dimensions: visual quality, text-to-video alignment, and physical consistency. Unlike existing single-score evaluators, VideoScore2 generates interpretable chain-of-thought rationales alongside numerical scores. The authors develop a large-scale dataset called VideoFeedback2 containing 27,168 human-annotated videos with scores and reasoning traces across all three dimensions. The model is trained via a two-stage pipeline combining supervised fine-tuning with reinforcement learning using Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to improve analytical robustness and human alignment. This enables comprehensive, explainable assessment of the multi-faceted aspects of generated video quality.
connects: This work extends the challenge of evaluating text-to-video generation systems, building on prior efforts to develop automated reward models and quality metrics for video content. It responds to limitations in existing evaluators that produce opaque single scores without interpretability or detailed analysis. The paper connects to broader research on explainable machine learning evaluation and human-aligned scoring systems, incorporating recent advances in chain-of-thought reasoning and reinforcement learning approaches like GRPO. VideoScore2 builds on the tradition of creating annotated evaluation datasets—here dramatically scaled up with 27,168 videos—and leverages multi-task learning to jointly optimize for accurate scoring and coherent reasoning, advancing the state of video quality assessment beyond monolithic metrics.
enables: VideoScore2 establishes infrastructure for more rigorous evaluation of text-to-video models during development and deployment, enabling researchers to identify specific weaknesses in generation systems across visual, semantic, and physical dimensions. The interpretable rationales support human-in-the-loop improvement of video generators and could guide targeted model refinement. The framework points toward future development of more nuanced evaluation approaches that decompose complex multi-modal quality assessment into understandable components. The VideoFeedback2 dataset becomes a resource for training future evaluators, and the two-stage training methodology combining supervised learning with GRPO optimization establishes a template for developing other explainable reward models in generative AI evaluation.
